name: table
title: Table
description: A static, semantic data table: a native <table> with caption, thead/tbody, th scope, and td. Composed primitives (Table.Head/Body/Row/HeaderCell/Cell) over real table semantics — not an ARIA grid. Supports bordered/borderless, striped, comfortable/compact density, opt-in per-column sorting (aria-sort + button + icon, consumer-controlled), and a stacked-card responsive mode. Wraps the table in a focusable, labelled scroll region for overflow.
useWhen: Displaying tabular data with relationships across rows and columns (records with shared attributes)
Data whose columns benefit from header association via scope (column and/or row headers)
Sortable columns where the consumer sorts the data and the component owns aria-sort + the sort control
avoidWhen: Page or section layout (use CSS grid/flex — never a table for visual arrangement)
A simple list of items with no cross-column relationships (use a list or the Collection component)
Spreadsheet-style cell navigation, inline editing, or focus-per-cell (that is a future interactive data grid using the ARIA APG Grid pattern — a native table is not a role=grid)

keyboard: A caption is REQUIRED — it names the table for screen readers and labels the scroll region; a missing caption is a dev-time warning
th uses scope="col" by default; pass scope="row" for row headers so cells associate with both axes

Sortable columns render a native <button> inside <th aria-sort="ascending|descending|none">; Tab reaches it, Enter or Space activates it, and sort direction is conveyed by aria-sort (non-color) plus a decorative icon
The horizontal scroll region is a focusable role=region (tabindex=0) labelled by the caption, so keyboard-only users can scroll overflowing columns
Stacked responsive mode keeps the real <table> in the DOM (only CSS display changes), so scope header associations survive; each cell supplies a label for its column name
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default — sortable header buttons are min-h-11 and full width
